question: What is the legal name of your company?
helperText: Provide the exact registered name of your company as per the documents.
guidanceTitle: Tips for Entering Company Name
guidanceTips: Enter the full legal name of your company as it appears on your GST registration, PAN card, or other official documents.
Avoid using abbreviations or short forms unless they are part of the registered name.
Double-check the spelling and capitalization to ensure it matches your official records.

question: What is your business registration number?
helperText: This is the unique identification number assigned to your business by the government. It is typically a combination of letters and numbers.
guidanceTitle: Providing your Business Registration Number
guidanceTips: The business registration number is typically a 10-15 digit alphanumeric code issued by the government, such as your GST registration number or PAN (Permanent Account Number).
Make sure to enter the exact number as it appears on your business registration documents, without any spaces or special characters.
If you are unsure of your business registration number, you can find it on official documents like your GST registration certificate, PAN card, or trade license.

question: What is your business's Goods and Services Tax (GST) registration number?
helperText: The GST number is a unique 15-digit identification number assigned to your business by the Government of India. This number is required for all business transactions involving the Goods and Services Tax.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for providing your GST number
guidanceTips: Ensure you enter the exact 15-digit GST number as it appears on your GST registration certificate.
The GST number follows a specific format: XX-AAAAA-NNNN-Z-Y-Z, where XX is the state code, AAAAA is the PAN-based business entity number, NNNN is a unique number, and Z and Y are check digits.
If you have recently registered for GST, make sure to use the new GST number and not the old VAT or service tax registration number.

question: What is your Permanent Account Number (PAN)?
helperText: PAN is a unique 10-digit alphanumeric number issued by the Income Tax Department. It is mandatory for all business transactions in India.
guidanceTitle: How to find and enter your PAN correctly
guidanceTips: Your PAN card contains your 10-digit PAN number. Ensure you enter it exactly as printed on the card.
PAN numbers always start with 5 uppercase letters followed by 4 digits and 1 uppercase letter.
Double-check your PAN number before submitting to avoid any errors or delays in processing your application.

question: What is the primary business type or industry of your organization?
helperText: Select the option that best describes your primary business activities.
guidanceTitle: Guidance on selecting the correct business type/industry
guidanceTips: Choose the industry that accounts for the majority of your revenue and operations.
If your business activities span multiple industries, select the one that is most relevant to this insurance policy.
Refer to your GST registration or PAN card to confirm your primary business classification.

question: What is the year your business was established?
helperText: Enter the 4-digit year your business was officially registered or started operations.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering Year of Establishment
guidanceTips: The year of establishment should be the year your business was officially registered or started operations, not the year you started planning the business.
If you're not sure of the exact year, provide your best estimate based on official records or founding documents.
The year should be a 4-digit number between 1900 and the current year. Do not enter a range of years.

question: What is the total number of employees in your organization?
placeholder: Enter the exact number of employees
helperText: Please provide the exact number of employees in your organization. This information is required to calculate the appropriate premium for your group personal accident insurance policy.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing the Number of Employees
guidanceTips: The number of employees should include all full-time, part-time, and contractual workers in your organization.
Ensure that the number you provide is accurate and up-to-date, as it directly impacts the premium calculation for your group personal accident insurance policy.
If you have any seasonal or temporary employees, include them in the total count as well.

question: What is your company's total annual revenue?
placeholder: Enter your annual revenue in INR
helperText: Please provide your company's total revenue for the last financial year. This information is required to calculate the appropriate insurance coverage and premium.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing Annual Revenue
guidanceTips: Enter the exact annual revenue figure, do not provide a range.
Ensure the revenue figure is for the most recent completed financial year.
If your company has multiple business units or subsidiaries, provide the consolidated annual revenue across all entities.

question: What is the complete address of your business?
helperText: Provide the full address including street, city, state, and PIN code.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering Business Address
guidanceTips: Ensure the address is accurate and matches your official business registration documents.
Include the complete address with building/flat number, street name, area/locality, city, state, and PIN code.
Double-check the spelling and formatting to avoid any errors or misinterpretations.

question: What is the city where your business is located?
helperText: Select the city where your business is located. This is required for accurate policy coverage and premium calculation.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Filling the City Field
guidanceTips: Provide the exact city where your business is headquartered or has its primary location.
If your business has multiple locations, select the city where the majority of your employees or operations are based.
Ensure the city name is spelled correctly and matches the legal address of your business.

question: In which state is your business located?
options: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
helperText: Select the state where your business is registered and operating.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Selecting the State
guidanceTips: Ensure you select the correct state where your business is headquartered or has its primary operations.
If your business has operations in multiple states, select the state where the majority of your employees or revenue is generated.
The state selected should match the address details provided in your business registration documents, such as GST registration or PAN card.

question: What is the PIN code of your business location?
helperText: The 6-digit PIN code is required to determine the geographic location of your business for insurance coverage and compliance purposes.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering the PIN Code
guidanceTips: The PIN code is a 6-digit number that uniquely identifies the postal area where your business is located.
You can find the PIN code for your business address on any utility bill, government document, or by searching online using your city/town name.
Ensure you enter the PIN code accurately as it is an important factor in determining the insurance premium and coverage for your group personal accident policy.

question: What is the name of the primary contact person for this insurance policy?
placeholder: Enter full name of contact person
helperText: Provide the full name of the person who will be the primary point of contact for this insurance policy.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering Contact Person Name
guidanceTips: Enter the full legal name of the contact person, including first, middle (if applicable), and last name.
Ensure the name matches the government-issued ID (such as PAN card or Aadhaar) of the contact person.
Avoid using abbreviations, nicknames, or initials in the name field.

question: What is your official business email address?
helperText: Please provide the email address registered for your business. This will be used for all policy-related communication.
guidanceTitle: Entering your business email address
guidanceTips: Use the official email address associated with your registered business entity.
Ensure the email address is spelled correctly and does not contain any typos.
Avoid using personal email addresses like Gmail or Yahoo. Use a professional, company-specific email domain.

question: What is your primary contact phone number?
helperText: Please provide a valid 10-digit mobile number starting with 6, 7, 8, or 9.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Phone Number
guidanceTips: Enter your primary mobile number where you can be easily reached.
Ensure the number is registered in your name and is currently active.
Avoid using landline numbers or shared/office numbers as the primary contact.

question: When would you like the policy to start?
helperText: The policy period is typically 1 year. Please select a start date within the next 12 months.
guidanceTitle: Selecting the Policy Start Date
guidanceTips: Choose a start date that aligns with your business needs and operations.
Ensure the start date is at least a few days after the current date to allow for policy processing.
Consider factors like your financial year, employee onboarding, or other insurance renewals when selecting the start date.

question: When would you like the policy to end?
helperText: The policy period is typically 1 year. Please select the desired end date for your coverage.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Selecting Policy End Date
guidanceTips: The policy end date should be 1 year from the start date of the coverage.
Ensure the end date falls within the current financial year (April 1st to March 31st).
Consider your business needs and the duration of coverage required for your employees.

question: What is the total coverage amount you require for your group personal accident insurance policy?
helperText: The coverage amount should be between ₹1,00,000 to ₹1,00,00,000 as per industry standards.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Determining Coverage Amount
guidanceTips: Consider the number of employees in your group and their average salaries to determine an appropriate coverage amount.
Higher coverage amounts provide better protection for your employees in case of accidental death or disability.
Review your industry's norms and competitors' offerings to ensure your coverage amount is competitive and meets regulatory requirements.

question: Who is your current group personal accident insurance provider?
placeholder: Enter the name of your current insurance provider
helperText: Provide the exact name of the insurance company currently providing your group personal accident coverage.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering Current Insurance Provider
guidanceTips: Enter the full legal name of the insurance company, not just an abbreviation or brand name.
If you have multiple group personal accident policies, provide the name of the primary insurance provider.
Ensure the information you provide matches your current insurance documents and certificates.

question: Please provide details of any previous claims made under any personal accident insurance policy in the last 5 years.
helperText: Provide a brief description of the nature of the claim, the amount claimed, and the outcome.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing Previous Claims History
guidanceTips: Be as specific as possible about the date, nature, and outcome of any previous claims.
Mention the insurance company and policy number under which the claim was made.
Provide the exact amount claimed and the amount settled, if applicable.
Explain the circumstances leading to the claim, such as the type of accident or injury.

question: Please describe the risk management measures in place for your business operations.
placeholder: Provide details on your risk mitigation strategies, safety protocols, and emergency response plans
helperText: Outline the key risk management practices you have implemented to ensure the safety and well-being of your employees and operations.
guidanceTitle: Guidance on Describing Risk Management Measures
guidanceTips: Highlight the specific measures you have in place to identify, assess, and mitigate risks in your business operations.
Provide details on your safety protocols, emergency response plans, and employee training programs.
Explain how your risk management approach aligns with industry best practices and regulatory requirements in India.
